text:
He has yet to earn his driver芒聙聶s license, but 16-year-old track phenom Quincy Wilson has earned himself a chance to go to theParis Olympicsthis summer.脗

Competing against elite athletes nearly twice his age in theU.S. Olympic track and field trialsin Eugene, Ore., the high school sophomore broke the under-18 world record in the 400 meters芒聙聰a mark that dated back 42 years芒聙聰when he ran a 44.66 in the first round on Friday. Then on Sunday, he surpassed his own mark with a 44.59 finish to advance to Monday芒聙聶s final.脗

In front of an energized crowd at Hayward Field on Monday night, Wilson, donning his singlet from Bullis High School in Potomac, Maryland, finished sixth in the final with a time of 44.94 seconds, denying him an automatic bid to Paris. Winner Quincy Hall finished in a personal-best 44.17 seconds to qualify, followed by Michael Norman (44.41) and Chris Bailey (44.42).

Quincy Hall DIGS DEEP down the homestretch to win the Olympic Trials 400m final in the fastest time by an American this year!#TrackFieldTrials24pic.twitter.com/iaeLxrH7Lp

While those three will represent the U.S. in the 400 in Paris in less than a month, Wilson芒聙聶s opportunity to join Team USA at the Summer Games is not lost. He could still become a 2024 Olympian if he is selected for the U.S. relay pool, a decision made by the men芒聙聶s relay coach Mike Marsh and a USA Track & Field selection panel after trials are complete. In addition to the three men who qualified for the individual 400-meter event, the U.S. can bring four more runners: two for the men芒聙聶s 4x400-meter relay and two for the mixed relay. For Tokyo, for example, the top seven finishers in the 400-meter final at U.S. trials were all selected as part of the relay pool.脗

芒聙聹I can芒聙聶t be disappointed,芒聙聺Wilson said after Monday芒聙聶s final. 芒聙聹At the end of the day, I芒聙聶m 16 years old running芒聙娄grown-man times.芒聙聺脗

If selected for the relay pool, Wilson will become the youngest American male to make the Olympic track and field team, and the first 16-year-old since Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone qualified for the Rio Games in 2016. Any preparation for that driver芒聙聶s license will also have to wait.脗

芒聙聹It芒聙聶s spectacular,芒聙聺 said Tokyo 4x400 relay gold medalist Norman, after the semifinal race. 芒聙聹A 16-year-old coming out here, competing like a true competitor, not letting the moment get too big but living in the moment.芒聙聺

title: Teen Track Star Quincy Wilson Can Still Achieve His Paris Olympics Dream
description: The teen phenom fell short of an automatic bid, but he still has a chance to earn a ticket to this summer芒聙聶s Games.
